long_description:
Backups that previously completed in ~29 hours now take 56+ hours or fail entirely.
Issue spans Classic and VPC Gen2 environments, suggesting systemic COS/network problem.
short_description: Severe backup performance degradation in IBM Cloud COS
close_notes:
service team initially balanced the worklod across the other US regions to proved some immediate bandwidth improvement
2 nodes were added to the cluster (with 2 more being configured) to provide a more permanent solution
current_status_next_steps:
Service teams are engaged and looking at 4 mitigation paths. Refer Recovery table
1 add additional capacity- Ongoing (3 nodes have been added and final one being configured)
2 adjust cross site weighting to shift load to dallas - completed, but monitoring for further adjustments
3 analyse current activity to identify heaviest users - completed
4 rolling restart of nodes across cluster (may not be needed)
customer_facing_impact:
Users may encounter highly increased wait times for COS stroage transfers for DB2 & Data backups.
Multiple enterprise clients cannot complete backups for over a month.
Backups that previously completed in ~29 hours now take 56+ hours or fail entirely.

Case: {Case#101759401} ##MPX node add fails with authentication errors




Slack(s):  #cos-cie / https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/C020XM6UTHN/p1765058766251559
MS Teams:  https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_MjExNzY2MDctMWFiNy00MTE4LWJmMjctODMzZTg1ZGVhNTcx%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%22fcf67057-50c9-4ad4-98f3-ffca64add9e9%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%220a1b11e6-ac3d-47f1-9b52-a6374aa278dd%22%7d


Times in UTC
[2025-DEC-06]
hh:mm AAA Impact Start .... sometime on 14th November
22:50 AAA Sev3 CIE Confirmed
21:45 AAA Ian Jack Joining as AVM OnDuty
22:12 SRE confirm that this is a maxed out capacity issue on some of their clusters. There is no imminent solution to provide additional capacity. 
22:13 COS LB & DCOPS  LB and DC Ops working on identifying 4 additional LB's to be racked and configured.  One has been found so far. 
22:27 COS & NETENG  team is looking in to any suspicious activity ("Top Talkers"). Team will review top talker data so we can have a higher degree of confidence that when we execute the other plans that we will not overrun the new / additional capacity. 
22:45 LB team is also working up a plan to change weighting on cross-region setup to see if we can shift a percentage of the load to DAL.  This would not affect the general MZR   Traffic just the cross-region stuff.   All due caution and exec oversight being out around the development and approval of the plan.
23:00 On "top talkers" - 60% of the load is sourced from DB2 but DB2 stating the traffic pattern and load has not change
23:12 A 4th option might be to recycle the LB nodes. This would be the last resort as it might not help and would be disruptive for other customers.
23:27 LB team are initiating a change to the weightings of the cross region traffic to favour DAL which might relieve the pressure in WDC
23:30 weighting change is completed. LB team are monitoring to see if this has helped.
23:35 The "Top  Talkers" team have ruled out any suspicious activity
23:40 increasing the weighting as the initial settings did not have the desired effect.
23:41 4 nodes have been identified to increase the capacity (taken from an expansion program). These will need to be physically reracked and configured. ETA for that will be 2-3 hours
23:50 starting 3rd attempt to increase the cross traffic weighting. each time increasing the weight towards DAL

07 Dec

00:05 With a weighting of 6, we are starting to see some slight improvement
00:16 requesting customer (AMC) to cancel their traffic which goes directly to COS (top talker). This would allow us to start the rolling restarts (until 10:30 PM EST )
01:40 Load balancers are now racked up and now waiting for port mapping.
01:52 Load balancers aggregate has gone down and with the recent changes the saturation on Dal has gone down. Spike in SJC and reduction is WDC 04 has been seen. team is still validating further
01:58 team sees traffic towards WDC09 which is now going to 20 seconds
02:01 port mapping for 2 nodes are provided which will be now started for cabling
02:02 Simultaneously team is verifying if there is drop in DB2 load  for 10.211.2.72 ip and 10.39.26.73
02:14 team sees alot of ADN traffic moving out of WDC04
02:27 SFP DAC cables are being checked to connect the 2 LBs with the available port connection
02:32 With the cross region load movement team doesn't see any abnormality going to any region/DC but sees quite effective improvement to WDC: currently monitoring
02:44 Team is still working to get the SFP DAC cables for the LBs and that shipment would be done by moving from different DC. The expected ETA: 20 mins
02:45 Since due to this activity the overall LB set up will not be done by 10:30 PM EST ( time to check with AMC: hence checking with AMC will be halted) 
03:14 Team is now moving all the required cables ( 8 DAC cables, total of 32 cables needed for setup) Initial setup may take another 20-30 minutes.
03:44 Technician has arrived and team is now in the process to setup the first LB: ETA 15 min. Meanwhile other cables are still coming up, team plans to ready one LB by then.
04:11 Team is still building the config and performing required paperwork for building up the LB.
04:49 Cable work for 2 LBs are now done, config and firmware update setup is going on.
05:14 Soeb Bohari will assist Donald in the configuration to speed up the process
05:40 3rd node has been cabled and is available for configuration
05:58 all the 4 Lbs are now cabled up, the config setup is still going for the 1st LB
06:18 Joshua G updated that AMC is starting to see some issues.
06:19 AVM checks if there can be some load adjustments made until the Lbs are available 
06:20 Team is checking if there are possible adjustments 
06:30 Team also checks on the LBs availability
06:35 LB team is preparing the switch port configuration
06:45 Sheik Ameen informed LB team will take 90-120 Mins to get the LBs ready to push into cluster. AVM checks if the plan is to move all 4 together or move one by one LB as they are ready to deploy.
06:50 Team will deploy all the 4 LBs altogether as soon they are configured
07:40 Joshua G needs to share an ETA with customer on by when we are targeting to have all the 4 LBs in clusters. Upon unanimous votes, 10 UTC has been set as the expected ETA.
08:15 AVM checks with Suparna to have a check with the LB team to ensure if 10 UTC is achievable or not
08:30 internal checkpoint has been set with Suparna and team to identify if by 10 UTC team seems progress towards setting up the LBs
08:58 LB team is running the automation scrips for the setup of LB, ETA 15-20 mins to know if that works. In case of any issues we might have to move to manual actions to complete the setup and decide on new ETA for the task completion.
09:25 With the automation steps proving unsuccessful, the team has transitioned to manual processing. Team will need an additional hour to evaluate their status. The next checkpoint is scheduled in one hour, and with a realistic outlook, the effort may require an additional 2–3 hours to complete.
10:28 The team is progressing well with the manual steps and is currently focused on licensing and node deployment. With this positive momentum, we expect to bring one node into the system—assuming no issues arise—and we should be able to provide an update on the remaining three nodes within the next 40 minutes.
11: 15 The preliminary configuration for one node has been completed. The validation process, including routing peering and connectivity checks, is about to commence. Our goal is to bring this node fully online. Upon successful completion, the same procedure will be applied to the remaining nodes.Next update 11:50 UTC
11:50 The sync process for the node has failed. Given the number of issues encountered, providing an accurate ETA is not feasible at this time. The team is working to provision additional bandwidth  and closer to that step, will share an update as soon as there is any progress.
13:00 management interface has been fixed, steam is working to get the node to rejoin the cluster.
13:48 Team is currently working on two nodes, both encountering the same issue where synchronization is not completing. There appears to be a communication problem with the management network, which is likely related to a firmware bug.
14:30 AVM raised a concern regarding the need to review all potential causes and to determine whether this behavior is typical during load balancer integration. It has been more than five hours since the manual actions were initiated, and we are still in the process of attempting to synchronize and bring the load balancer into the cluster.
14:39 LB team: this is not an issue the team typically encounters. They are continuing to work diligently to resolve the synchronization problem and are conducting a thorough review of all potential root causes. All relevant components and dependencies are being assessed to ensure no contributing factors are overlooked.
14:40 LB team is checking all the possible approaches from their side and if none of those results positive, they might have to include the vendor, and that can consume more time to go from level 1 support.
14:54 LB team is working to engage Netscaler vendor support
15:09 Meanwhile COS team stated that they are monitoring the WDC env and donot see any abnormality to report.
15:14 Netscaler has joined the call and getting briefing of the issue ({Case#101759401} ##MPX node add fails with authentication errors)
15:53 with the assistance of vendor support New node ( node 8 ) is added into cluster .. is is starting to get traffic. Team is monitoring.
16;01 Team is also checking on the other 3 nodes and checking the issues identified with vendor support to ix this.
16:20 Team found that ESR and the three backup buckets were under heavy load. In addition, there was notable activity associated with cloud log data, contributing further to the overall strain.
16:22 Vlan tagging issue is being checked with vendor and team is sharing the actions performed and where they found issues. It was suspected that vlan tagging issue could be the reason of getting synchronization issues.
16:26 Another support eng from vendor has joined the call
16:28 Node 9 is stuck on synchronization error and being checked with vendor and being worked upon to active into cluster.
16:29 Team is planning to simultaneously deploy other 2 nodes in passive state and validate if those run into sync issues  ( which is quite likely to happen)  can also be checked from the vendor end with no delay.
17:11 Node 9 is being vetted to be added in cluster as sync issues are now fixed.Other 2 nodes being initiated for joining process and to check if sync issues occurs for them.
17;17 Node 9 is in upstate and team is trying to get the BGP for that. meanwhile the other 2 nodes are in the initial process to add them inthe cluster.
17:41 Node 9 has been pushed to cluster and being monitored.Other 2 nodes are currently being worked on.
17:55 Traffic shaping has been reverted
18:00 DB2 are running some tests to see if their performance has stablised
18:30 Node10 has been added to the cluster. Continuing to work on the final node
18:58 Node 11 has been aded to the cluster. Performance is looking stable


=============================

               FOR AVM REFERENCE
=============================

AVM team are to email Anisat Simmons with a status update on the 4 action plans by 08:00 EST (13:00 UTC) 7th December, or if there is any significant change in the status of the CIE.
